UC campuses — signing saturation by department

Built from a near-comprehensive census of UC faculty rosters. The Letter toggle picks which ucstudentsuccess.org open letter counts as signing — the STEM letter, the Social Sciences/Humanities/Professional‑Schools letter, or both combined (signers of both are counted once). Pick a campus, then drag the slider (or press play) to watch each unit's saturation — the share of its faculty who had signed — accumulate over the signing sequence. Tap a bar to see the names behind it; in trajectory view, tap a line to isolate it. The faculty base control sets who counts, in both numerator and denominator.

Campus
Letter

How to read this page — methodology & caveats
Reading it. The Letter toggle selects the numerator: STEM-letter signatures, SocSci/Hum/Professional-letter signatures, or both letters combined (a person signing both counts once; the combined time axis interleaves the two letters by % progress through each, which is synthetic — the letters launched at different times). Snapshot bars show saturation as of the chosen signature #; trajectory lines show each unit climbing across the sequence. Click a bar (or “names” in the line-isolation chip) to open the roster panel: signers in bold, in signing order, then non-signers in gray, alphabetically. Denominators come from the faculty census (harvested campus catalogs, department pages, and directories), not from UC headcount statistics; joint appointments are counted once, in the primary listing. The faculty-base toggle filters both sides consistently: “Ladder + teaching” approximates the Senate professoriate, “+ emeriti” matches the UCSD deep-dive page's catalog-core definition, and “All listed” adds lecturers, clinical, adjunct, research, visiting and affiliated entries. Signers whose rank falls outside the chosen base are excluded from both numerator and denominator (the line above the slider reports how many are shown). Department bars require at least 5 faculty in the current base and at least one signer; the “By field” and STEM groupings are a full census of every harvested unit. Fields are classified from department names; STEM = math/statistics, computing, engineering, physical and biological sciences; non-STEM = social sciences, humanities/arts, economics/business, and law/policy/education. Medical/health and unclassified units are omitted from the STEM split. Units marked † had no usable rank information in the harvest, so their full roster is used under every faculty base. Senate service splits the campus census by whether the person sat on a systemwide Academic Senate committee in 2016-17 to 2025-26 (universitywide rosters — a much rarer form of service than divisional committee work). Divisional service is the same split using the campus's own divisional Academic Senate committee rosters over the same window, assembled from campus Senate sites and documents, Internet Archive captures, the UCLA Senate Data Management System, UCR previous-membership pages, and the UCSD membership tool. All nine undergraduate campuses are covered, but roster completeness varies by year: the coverage chip above the chart reports, for the selected campus, how many of the ten window years have full rosters and where the gaps are. Missing years can only under-flag service — the “divisional committee” group is a floor, and the “no divisional role” group may include people whose service fell in an uncovered year. Prior letters splits the census by whether the person signed the 2021 “Replace the Proposed California Math Curriculum Framework” or “Open Letter on K-12 Mathematics” letters. Both splits count only people matched into the campus census: committee members or letter signers who are no longer on any harvested roster (or sit in un-harvested units) are not counted, so the flagged groups are conservative.
